title: Inorder Traversal

starterCode.python:
# Definition for a  binary tree node

class Solution:
	# @param A : root node of tree
	# @return a list of integers
	def inorderTraversal(self, A):

hints: Inorder traversal visits nodes in the order: left, root, right.
Use an iterative approach with a stack to simulate the recursive call stack.
Keep going left until you reach null, then pop from stack, add to result, and go right.
The algorithm: while current node is not null or stack is not empty, if current is not null, push and go left, else pop, add to result, and go right.
You can also use recursion, but the iterative approach avoids stack overflow for deep trees.
